issues.claim: engineering layer's operational scoping of Sanskrit terminology
issues.objection: The engineering rendering of BG 2.55 narrows the Sanskrit's scope (Shankara's gloss preserved: the operator-state where contentment is intrinsic, not built from external supply chain — engineering layer stays at operational level (does not claim ātman-as-Brahman absorption)) to a specifically operational frame. Acceptable simplification — the source pack preserves the fuller commentarial scope — but a careful Sanskritist notes the narrowing.

notes: The engineering layer for BG 2.55 preserves the verse's structural shape: Shankara's gloss preserved: the operator-state where contentment is intrinsic, not built from external supply chain — engineering layer stays at operational level (does not claim ātman-as-Brahman absorption). Vishishtadvaita reading (highest jñāna-bhakti / mind depending on inner self) is not directly invoked but not contradicted; engineering layer remains tradition-neutral on the metaphysical anchor. Dvaita reading (relation to Supreme as content of contentment) preserved at neutral level. No tradition is misattributed, no doctrinally load-bearing word is dropped without acknowledgment. The Sanskrit terms (sthita-prajña / sthita-dhīḥ / samādhi-sthasya / kūrma / rasa / param) appear preserved verbatim where invoked. PASS.
